response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries which served as the starting point for their collection. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily meaning he had 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia indicating that Sophia had 13 blueberries. The total number of blueberries collected by all three was 24 plus 39 plus 13 which equals 76 blueberries. These blueberries were used to make muffins which required 9 blueberries per batch.

***

They used the 76 blueberries to make muffins. Dividing the total number of blueberries by the number needed per batch gave them 8 full batches with some blueberries left over. Each batch was sold for $5 so multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch gave them a total of $40. This was the amount of money they earned from selling the muffins. The leftover blueberries were not used for making additional batches.

***

The process of collecting and selling the blueberries involved several steps. First they gathered the blueberries then they prepared them for baking. After making the muffins they sold each batch at a fixed price. The money earned from the sales was the result of their efforts and the value of the blueberries they had collected. This money represented the outcome of their combined work.

***

The blueberries collected by Emily David and Sophia were transformed into muffins which were then sold. The sale of these muffins brought in a total of $40. This money was the result of their hard work and the value of the blueberries they had gathered. The youth of the group was evident in their ability to work together and create something valuable from their resources.
